mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 5.5% indicates weakening labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings visibility and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the HOUST-RSAFS composite falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns are negative despite concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening corporate capacity utilization, which reduces earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 3.0% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) in a low-unemployment environment signals labor market tightness, which drives wage inflation and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or 20-day returns are positive despite tight labor market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) indicates economic recession, which triggers risk-off sentiment and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 declines by > 1.0% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening, which raises equity discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.
